spec_match.rationale: Music player popout with copyStyleSheets — the canonical use case the working group called out.

interactive.rationale: Real popout opens; media controls visible inside the PiP window.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises music-player now-playing with design-system carrying — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show MediaSession API integration (lock-screen controls are typically driven by MediaSession). The slug suggests lock-screen but the actual surface is the floating PiP window.

references.rationale: See also links to chromestatus.com/feature/5111711352684544 — WRONG feature ID. This belongs to feature 6275022143094784. Critical rule #3 regression.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, design system tokens carry through.

openQuestions.title: Fix the wrong chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: See also points at chromestatus.com/feature/5111711352684544 but this concept belongs to feature 6275022143094784. Critical fix.

openQuestions.title: Wire MediaSession action handlers
openQuestions.detail: The slug `lock-screen-controls` suggests integration with the OS lock-screen / media keys. Add `navigator.mediaSession.setActionHandler` for play/pause/next/previous so the OS surfaces the controls properly.

summary: Music-player popout demonstrating the WG's motivating use case. Ships the WRONG chromestatus feature ID and the slug-promised lock-screen integration via MediaSession isn't actually wired.
